name: Tyler Technologies Odyssey Court E-Filing API
what_it_does: Tyler Technologies Odyssey court case management and e-filing REST API for electronic court filing, case management, and court records access for attorneys, legal professionals, and court agencies. Enables AI agents to manage electronic court document filing and acceptance for legal case e-filing automation, handle case docket and status lookup for litigation tracking automation, access court calendar and hearing date retrieval for legal scheduling automation, retrieve case document and filing retrieval for legal document management automation, manage court fee calculation and payment processing for filing fee automation, handle service of process and attorney service notification for litigation service automation, access judge assignment and courtroom scheduling for litigation workflow automation, retrieve case history and docket entry for legal research automation, manage attorney and firm credential management for court portal access automation, and integrate Odyssey with law practice management systems, legal billing platforms, and case management software for end-to-end court workflow integration.
use_cases: Filing court documents from litigation automation agents
Tracking case status from docket monitoring agents
Scheduling hearings from legal calendar automation agents
Retrieving court records from case research agents
not_for: Arbitration and alternative dispute resolution platforms
best_when: A law firm, legal service provider, or legal operations team wanting AI agents to automate state court e-filing, case status monitoring, hearing calendar management, and court record retrieval across Tyler Odyssey-powered courts.
avoid_when: COURT JURISDICTION ODYSSEY DEPLOYMENT VARIATION: Tyler Odyssey is deployed across hundreds of state and county courts but with jurisdiction-specific configuration; automated e-filing workflows must verify Odyssey filing rules for specific court (accepted document formats, required fields, filing fees); automated filing with incorrect court-specific configuration creates rejection and deadline risk. LEGAL DEADLINE ACCURACY FOR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS: Automated court e-filing for deadline-critical documents (SOL complaints, responsive pleadings) must verify court filing deadline calculation including court holiday calendar and business day rules; automated deadline miscalculation creating missed filing creates malpractice liability. UNAUTHORIZED PRACTICE OF LAW FOR AUTOMATED FILING: Automated court document preparation and filing must be supervised by licensed attorney; automated legal document drafting and filing without attorney review and authorization creates unauthorized practice of law in all US jurisdictions.

auth.notes: Tyler Technologies Odyssey uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON and XML. Plano, Texas HQ (Tyler Technologies). NYSE: TYL. S&P 500. Odyssey is Tyler's flagship court case management system deployed in 500+ courts nationwide. Electronic filing hub (EFileIL, FileAndServeXpress integration). ECF/USTP integration. Court fee payment processing. Civil, criminal, family, probate court support. Competes with Thomson Reuters eCourt, ImageSoft, and Journal Technologies for court management systems.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — court agency licensing; attorney access via court-specific filing fee
pricing.paid_tiers: Court agency: annual license by court size; Attorney/firm: per-filing fee to court; API integration: Tyler developer partner program with per-transaction fees
pricing.notes: Plano TX. NYSE: TYL. Court agency annual license. Attorney per-filing court fees. Tyler developer partner API access program. Per-transaction integration fees.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; filing submission and case lookup throttled; no webhooks — polling required for filing status

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — FILING STATUS REQUIRES POLLING: Tyler Odyssey does not support webhooks for filing acceptance events; automated filing status detection (accepted, rejected, pending) requires scheduled polling; implement polling with legal deadline urgency awareness for time-sensitive filings (same-day deadline, emergency motions)

COURT-SPECIFIC CONFIGURATION VARIATION: Tyler Odyssey is deployed with court-specific configuration; accepted document formats (PDF/A, TIFF), required filing fields, service types, and fee schedules vary by court; automated filing workflow must retrieve court-specific rules before submission; generic filing template will fail for specific court requirements

LEGAL DEADLINE COURT CALENDAR DEPENDENCY: Automated legal deadline calculation must integrate court-specific holiday calendar; court filing deadlines that fall on court holidays or weekends have jurisdiction-specific rollforward rules (FRCP Rule 6 vs state rules); automated deadline without court calendar creates miscalculated filing deadline and malpractice risk

ATTORNEY SUPERVISION GATE FOR AUTOMATED FILING: Court e-filing automation must include attorney review and approval gate before submission; automated document drafting and filing without attorney review and authorization creates unauthorized practice of law; implement mandatory attorney sign-off in automated legal workflow

TYLER DEVELOPER PARTNER PROGRAM ENROLLMENT REQUIRED: Tyler Odyssey API access requires enrollment in Tyler Technologies developer partner program; API access is not self-serve; partner program review and approval may take weeks; plan for Tyler partner program onboarding timeline before building court automation

SEALED AND CONFIDENTIAL CASE ACCESS RESTRICTIONS: Automated case lookup and document retrieval must check case seal status before returning records; sealed, confidential, and expunged case records have restricted access; automated bulk case research without seal status check creates unauthorized access to restricted records
agent_readiness.security_notes: Court e-filing. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Court records, case data, and attorney credential data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Court management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Court records, legal filings, and case data.
